name: Benny Goodman
abstract: Benjamin David Goodman (May 30, 1909 – June 13, 1986) was an American jazz clarinetist and bandleader, known as the "King of Swing". From 1935 until the mid-1940s, Goodman led one of the most popular swing big bands in the United States.

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Benny Goodman in the context of answering a trivia question about Fletcher Henderson and mentions a third party (George Duvivier) who had a 'run-in' with Goodman, but no personal interaction or direct professional relationship between Vincent Pelote and Benny Goodman is des
about_quotes.doc_title: Vincent Pelote interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 25, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Benny Goodman in the context of his friend Mel Powell's history ('hired by Benny Goodman to replace Teddy Wilson') and historical events ('Goodman Breaks the Color Barrier'), but never claims a personal interaction or direct professional relationship with Goodman himself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Roland Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: So I went up to Benny Goodman’s house... But anyway I went up and played for him... And then we had rehearsals... And then I did the tour... I was still off and on with Benny until he hired me on a regular basis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roland Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Claude Williams
about_quotes.evidence: And I hear Benny Goodman picked up the thing one night. And Benny told his brother-in-law which was John Hammond about the group, and John Hammond checked it and he went down to Kansas City and listened to it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Claude Williams interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Goodman had this guitarist named Charlie Christian... Charlie Christian because he was with Benny Goodman, you know, became a name like Lionel Hampton and Teddy Wilson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Stuart Bergen
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was an Italian guy who used to arrange for Benny Goodman. And they invited that guy to their house... And he gave them all copies of Benny Goodman's arrangements.
